outcome_detail: CrossHair FOUND the postcondition violation: 'error: false when calling double_it(8.98846567431158e+307) (which returns float("inf"))', exit code 1. The expected outcome was exit 0 / not confirmed; the infrequent IEEE-faithful floating-point channel fired on this minimal single-operation target. Recorded as-is per instruction: a confirmation measures the FP channel's width. Not re-run.
mechanism_attribution: Class (2) model-fidelity incompleteness, not search-budget (framing per closeout ruling R3). CrossHair 0.0.107 predominantly models Python floats as mathematical reals, attempting IEEE-faithful floating-point execution only infrequently (changelog v0.0.72); nan/+inf/-inf are injected as float ARGUMENTS only (v0.0.58), so a computed-intermediate overflow from finite inputs is reachable only through the IEEE-faithful channel. That channel exists but is unreliably sampled and sharply complexity-dependent: under identical invocation and bounds it confirmed a violation on this one-operation probe (this pin) yet stayed silent on the four-parameter kernel (exhibit_pin_naive_widening.json) - the two pins are a paired measurement. Nothing in the recorded bounds discloses which regime a run sat in. Changelog: https://crosshair.readthedocs.io/en/stable/changelog.html (v0.0.72, v0.0.58).
crosshair_version_source: pip show crosshair-tool -> 'Name: crosshair-tool' / 'Version: 0.0.107' (crosshair CLI 0.0.107 has no --version flag)
invocation_source: run_manifest_overflow_probe.json 'command' field; no bounds flags passed, CrossHair defaults in effect (same invocation shape as the amendment runs)
deterministic_companion: tests/test_overflow_probe.py asserts math.isinf(double_it(1e308)) - the violation as executable fact (1 passed).
version_contingency: Both the probe confirmation and the Sample C miss are version-contingent; claims are scoped to the pinned version above.
